text:
Market today: Chips are the story. Micron's blowout earnings are lifting the whole semiconductor space — that's a tailwind for your heaviest exposure. Oil moving up on Strait of Hormuz tension adds macro noise but isn't your core risk today.

Your holdings: PLTR is your loudest problem — down 16.7% and flagged SELL; on margin that hole gets deeper fast, and it's sitting well above your $85 stop with room to keep bleeding. AVGO is your largest position, down 12.6% and flagged TRIM — even with today's chip lift, this one needs a size reduction, not patience. SPCX is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with almost no trading history — tiny move to your $150 stop, margin amplifies any gap-down overnight.

Watch: 1) Does Micron's momentum actually lift AVGO and SMH meaningfully today — that's your clearest relief valve. 2) Oil/Hormuz escalation: sustained spike would pressure tech multiples broadly.

Bottom line: Cut or trim PLTR and AVGO before the margin math makes the decision for you.
